title: Implement byte-read + preallocation in Rust load

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
Linked story: virt-46d4a8
Goal: switch `Table::load_from_dir` to `fs::read` + `serde_json::from_slice` and pre-allocate record/manifest capacity based on file count.
Benchmark gate: compare 10k `full_database_cold_load` vs Rust baseline (2929.900 ms) and Python baseline (3354.440 ms).

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
## Experiment: byte reads + preallocation
Change: `fs::read` + `serde_json::from_slice`, pre-allocate `records`/`manifest` based on file count.

### Bench results (10k `full_database_cold_load`, 5 runs)
- Rust (attempt): runs [3023.481, 2674.395, 2884.114, 2766.328, 4762.610]
- mean **3222.186 ms**, median **2884.114 ms**
- Python runs [2983.267, 3069.394, 2945.505, 3091.563, 2992.783]
- mean **3016.502 ms**, median **2992.783 ms**

### Decision
No clear improvement over Rust baseline (median roughly unchanged; mean worse due outlier). Reverted the change.
